excerpt: When all these qualities are combined in a person, he becomes one of the nobles, worshippers and saints of Allah.

The essence of obedience is the liberation of the self from all desires. The way to do this is to forbid 'Abd from acquiring for himself all the things he desires, and to persuade him to endure the pain of all that he dislikes. And the essence of this action is that a person should abandon rest, prefer solitude and follow the path of the knowledge of Allah.

When the gaze is directed towards something forbidden, a knot of desire is tied in the heart of a person. The knot can only be opened in two cases: Either a person sheds tears of sorrow and sorrow while sincerely repenting. Or take ownership of the object or desire he was looking at.
source_location_hint: Section 2 (Lowering the Gaze), on forbidden gazes and repentance

excerpt: Know that a little knowledge requires a lot of action, because it is necessary for a person to act on the knowledge acquired in an hour for the rest of his life. Jesus said, 'I saw a stone on which it was written, Turn me back! When I turned it over, it was written on the other side: Whoever does not act on his knowledge will be punished by having to search for knowledge for the rest of his life (to clear his doubts), and his current acquired knowledge will be turned against him.
It is permissible to make a decision only for a person who has been endowed with the following qualities: Inner Purity, Sincerity in Outward and Inward Actions, A clear proof from his Lord.
source_location_hint: Section 5 (Making Judgment), on qualifications for judgment

excerpt: He should be a good advocate for people, kind and compassionate. He should understand the different temperaments of the people and invite them to the truth with gentle and clear arguments so that everyone can benefit from his advice. He should be aware of the desires of the soul and the snares of Satan. Be patient in the face of adversity.
source_location_hint: Section 6 (Commanding Good and Forbidding Evil), on manner of counsel

excerpt: Be like the merciful healer who places the merciful in the place where he benefits the most. Someone asked him, 'O Spirit of Allah, whose company should we take?' He said, 'With the one whose existence reminds you of Allah, whose words increase your knowledge, and whose deeds make you yearn for the Hereafter.'
source_location_hint: Section 7 (Ruin of Scholars), on choice of companions

excerpt: True gratitude can be expressed in such a way that a person sincerely asks for forgiveness for his inability to be grateful for any of the blessings of Allaah. Therefore, it is obligatory for him to recite Allah's Takbeer, Tahmeed and Tahamid.
When you return home after achieving your goal, enter it in the same way as a corpse enters a grave, whose purpose is only to seek Allah's mercy and forgiveness.
source_location_hint: Section 10 (Departing from Home), on returning home

excerpt: Get your sleep not like a careless person, but like a conscious person...Sleep with the intention that you are reducing your burden on the angels and cutting off your self from your desires. Get a lesson for your life from your sleep!

Make the remembrance of Allah a wonderful part of your thoughts, just as Allah remembers you at all times...The knowledge that you gain from the remembrance of Allah and His remembrance will instill in you humility, modesty, and remorse.
source_location_hint: Section 23 (Remembrance of Allah), on dhikr and knowledge
